customers.topSignal: ENG-9652: GetData v.s. GetStructuredData query perf analysis

decisions.title: Adopt DataFusion for CSRS query engine to improve GetData performance
decisions.context: ENG-9652 reveals perf gap between GetData and GetStructuredData. ENG-9493/9460 implement DataFusion reading for DLv2/v3 parquet. CSRS caching (ENG-9457/9789) + manifest fetching (ENG-9456) enable faster evidence retrieval. Aligns with 'Nail the Core' - making Explore V2 performant at scale.

arguments.for: DataFusion proven for columnar analytics. Caching layer + new parquet schema = 3-5x query speedup. Unblocks enterprise customers hitting query timeouts. Directly supports North Star by making decisions faster to reach.
arguments.against: Migration risk: breaks existing query patterns. Requires dual-read support during transition (ENG-9423 multi-channel spike needed). Team capacity stretched with compaction work (ENG-9414). May delay E2 GA (ENG-9779).

decisions.title: Prioritize SCIM reliability fixes over new SSO features
decisions.context: ENG-9707 reports random user deactivations in production. ENG-9783 highlights lack of SCIM testing. ENG-9584 SSO error UX is secondary to data integrity. 'Embed Deeper' requires enterprise auth stability. Active escalations from 2 design partners.
decisions.blockedBy: No reliable SCIM test environment - ENG-9783 unscheduled, needs infra resources from DevOps (committed to ENG-9778 load testing through April)

arguments.for: User deactivation = broken enterprise trust. SCIM is table stakes for 'Embed Deeper' in Fortune 500. Test infra investment prevents future escalations. Aligns with 'Build the Moat' - competitors don't have SCIM issues.
arguments.against: ENG-8751 user reactivation and ENG-9584 error UX affect broader user base. SCIM issues isolated to 2 customers. Test env buildout = 2-3 week distraction from feature roadmap. Canvas launch (ENG-9438) at risk if IAM team diverted.

decisions.title: Defer Canvas Status Summary (ENG-9438) to bundle with Candidate Runs feature
decisions.context: ENG-9531 (FE) and ENG-9529 (BE) for Candidate Runs in flight. ENG-9438 status summary overlaps UX. Canvas is 'Build the Moat' bet - should launch with differentiated ML workflow, not incremental status widgets. E2 GA (ENG-9779) takes priority.

arguments.for: Bundling = stronger launch narrative ('Canvas shows you which runs matter'). Avoids half-baked release. Eng capacity redirected to E2 GA feature flags audit and run link bugs (ENG-9601). Candidate Runs backend ready week of 4/7, can ship together 4/14.
arguments.against: Status summary already 60% complete, deferral wastes sunk cost. Design partners expect it in April preview. Candidate Runs may slip (depends on ENG-9648 archive review). Delaying visible progress risks stakeholder confidence in Canvas.

decisions.title: Invest in Policy API validation endpoint before public API release
decisions.context: ENG-9398 flags missing validation for Python Rules API. 'Nail the Core' means Sift Edge policies must be reliable. Without validate endpoint, users push broken policies to production. Ask SIFT integration blocked until policy contracts stable.

arguments.for: Validation = 80% of support tickets prevented (based on Rules V1 data). Required for API versioning strategy. Enables self-serve 'Build the Moat' - users trust programmatic policy updates. 3-day effort, high ROI.
arguments.against: Policy API already in private beta with 5 customers, no validation complaints yet. ENG-9414 compaction throughput more urgent for scale. Validation can be added in V1.1 after GA. Team wants to ship Python Rules announce at user conference (5/12).

decisions.title: Standardize histogram rendering on Canvas (ENG-5647) before Explore V2 chart redesign
decisions.context: ENG-6409 plots families in histograms (Explore). ENG-5647 creates alt canvas renderer. Two chart systems = fragmented UX. 'Nail the Core' requires consistent visual language. Canvas renderer 2x faster, supports real-time updates.

arguments.for: Canvas renderer future-proof (WebGL), current SVG histograms choke on 10k+ points. Unified codebase reduces maintenance. ENG-9776 sharedWorker + cache enables canvas perf. Family histogram UX improved for both products.
arguments.against: ENG-5647 in playground, not production-ready. Migration breaks existing histogram integrations (notebooks, embeds). ENG-6409 needed for Q2 ML team commitments. Renderer switch = 2 sprint delay, pushes Explore V2 to June.

keyResults.name: Python Rules API GA adoption 40 customers

timeAudit.weekSummary: High meeting density driven by E2 GA planning (5 sessions), SCIM incident retros (2), and Canvas design reviews (3). Backend platform stretched across CSRS refactor, compaction, and SCIM fires - recommend consolidating ENG-9456/9457/9460/9493 into single 2-week sprint vs. parallel work. IAM team underwater: ENG-9707/9783/9584/8751/9731 span 4 epics with 1.5 engineers.
alerts: Backend Platform eng capacity at 140% (CSRS DataFusion migration + compaction + Kafka consumer work)
IAM team handling prod incidents (SCIM) while building new features (Keycloak refactor) - recommend feature freeze until ENG-9707 resolved
No owner assigned to ENG-9783 SCIM testing - blocks decision #2, needs exec escalation
ENG-9601 E2 launch blocker (run links broken) - only 1 engineer assigned, no target date
ENG-9776 sharedWorker impacts multiple teams (Canvas renderer, Explore charts) but no PM coordination visible
